behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/REAL-INFRA: failure can occur at the CLI↔real git subprocess boundary (rename detection flags/thresholds). MOCK-HIDE: a fake git response with ideal rename records would pass even if real git output classifies the same change as add+delete. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Integration->System): integration test stubs git client to return `R100 old -> new`, parser passes; real run without proper rename-detection options yields `A new` + `D old`, so behavior fails. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text is about actual renamed files in a repository report, which depends on real git subprocess classification, not only in-process module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI against that repo, and assert report has the file only in `renamed` with no matching `added`/`deleted` double-count.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: binary diff handling depends on real git diff/subprocess output and encoding/stream behavior. MOCK-HIDE: text fixtures often omit true binary metadata/edge bytes and can miss decode/crash paths. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Integration->System): parser test with mocked `Binary files differ` line passes; real subprocess output/decoding path raises UnicodeDecodeError or crashes on NUL-containing data. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires handling real diffs that include binary files without crashing.
behaviors.test_description: In a temporary real git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run CLI, assert process exits successfully and binary file contributes to category counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: unborn-HEAD behavior is a real git subprocess exit/status condition. MOCK-HIDE: mocked git calls usually return success payloads and miss real `fatal`/exit-code handling. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Integration->System): integration fake returns empty change list and tool passes; real `git` command on no-commit repo returns non-zero and tool incorrectly surfaces error. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly targets repository state "no commits yet", which is encoded in real git command behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, run CLI, assert zero exit and empty structured report (not traceback/error output).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this validates end-to-end correctness against actual repository history, not isolated function logic. REAL-INFRA: requires real git history traversal and subprocess interaction. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Integration->System): integration test injects precomputed change tuples and aggregator counts correctly; real repo with mixed renames/deletes/binaries yields different raw git results and report counts drift. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text says counts must match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo history with known added/deleted/renamed/modified events across commits, run CLI, and compare reported counts to independently computed expected counts from the repo history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: staged vs unstaged is git index/working-tree state handled by real git and filesystem state. MOCK-HIDE: stubs can force identical payloads and hide index-related divergence bugs. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Integration->System): mocked repository adapter returns same change set for both modes so test passes; real git commands differ (`--cached` vs working tree) and tool outputs mismatch. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly compares real staged and unstaged repository states.
behaviors.test_description: In a temporary real repo, make equivalent file changes once staged and once unstaged (in controlled runs), execute CLI for each mode, and assert byte-identical structured stdout.
